content:
MARKET OVERVIEW

1. What is the Current Market Regime?
RISK-ON: normal volatility (Vol: 27.1%) with broad participation (Breadth: 7/8 sectors positive). Market tone favors cyclical recovery rather than defensive shelter.

2. Which sectors are leading?
Holding & Investment and Energy & Chemicals are leading on the week, driven by DOHOL (1W: +6.94% | 1M: -3.63%) RECOVERY and TUPRS (1W: +2.13% | 1M: +18.00%) ABOVE TREND. Construction names like TKFEN (1W: +15.25% | 1M: -0.58%) RECOVERY are also running hard on momentum.

3. Which sectors are lagging?
Banking & Finance is the clear laggard with systemic downside pressure: AKBNK (1W: -2.03% | 1M: -20.41%) BELOW TREND, GARAN (1W: -1.99% | 1M: -18.68%) BELOW TREND and VAKBN (1W: -4.54% | 1M: -20.19%) BELOW TREND. Retail shows sharp dispersion—ULKER (1W: -5.87% | 1M: -21.64%) BELOW TREND and SOKM (1W: -8.28% | 1M: -18.15%) BELOW TREND are weak despite sector-level gains.

STOCK PERFORMANCE

4. Top Performing Stocks?
TKFEN (1W: +15.25% | 1M: -0.58%) RECOVERY, BIMAS (1W: +13.85% | 1M: +0.78%) ABOVE TREND, SASA (1W: +10.81% | 1M: -9.23%) RECOVERY, PETKM (1W: +9.46% | 1M: +4.84%) ABOVE TREND, TOASO (1W: +7.07% | 1M: -6.05%) RECOVERY.

5. Worst Performing Stocks?
GARAN (1W: -1.99% | 1M: -18.68%) BELOW TREND, AKBNK (1W: -2.03% | 1M: -20.41%) BELOW TREND, OYAKC (1W: -2.04% | 1M: -15.43%) BELOW TREND, ASELS (1W: -3.37% | 1M: +6.88%) CORRECTION, ALARK (1W: -3.49% | 1M: -21.23%) BELOW TREND.

6. Momentum Stocks?
Upside momentum concentrated in TKFEN (1W: +15.25% | 1M: -0.58%) RECOVERY, BIMAS (1W: +13.85% | 1M: +0.78%) ABOVE TREND and SASA (1W: +10.81% | 1M: -9.23%) RECOVERY; watch follow-through. Downside momentum flagged by SOKM (1W: -8.28% | 1M: -18.15%) BELOW TREND as the largest weekly decliner.

SECTOR OUTLOOK

7. Banking Sector Outlook?
Banking remains under pressure and is the primary laggard; AKBNK (1W: -2.03% | 1M: -20.41%) BELOW TREND, GARAN (1W: -1.99% | 1M: -18.68%) BELOW TREND and HALKB (1W: -4.33% | 1M: -13.50%) BELOW TREND show broad weakness. Positioning: defensive stance until signs of base formation or macro relief.

8. Industrial Sector Outlook?
Heavy industry is mixed but improving on select names: EREGL (1W: +0.71% | 1M: -5.02%) RECOVERY, FROTO (1W: +1.11% | 1M: -13.76%) RECOVERY and TOASO (1W: +7.07% | 1M: -6.05%) RECOVERY are leading, while ASELS (1W: -3.37% | 1M: +6.88%) CORRECTION needs monitoring for short-term pullback risk.

9. Energy & Chemicals Outlook?
Energy shows constructive breadth: TUPRS (1W: +2.13% | 1M: +18.00%) ABOVE TREND, PETKM (1W: +9.46% | 1M: +4.84%) ABOVE TREND and SASA (1W: +10.81% | 1M: -9.23%) RECOVERY are driving the sector; OYAKC (1W: -2.04% | 1M: -15.43%) BELOW TREND is the notable lag.

PORTFOLIO STRATEGY

10. Portfolio Recommendation?
Pro-risk tilt: trim bank exposure and reallocate into selective cyclicals—energy, holding groups and industrials showing weekly strength. Use earnings/trading triggers to scale into BIMAS (ABOVE TREND) and TUPRS (ABOVE TREND) and keep stop discipline on recovery plays.

NEXT WEEK OUTLOOK

11. Stocks to Watch Next Week?
TUPRS (1W: +2.13% | 1M: +18.00%) ABOVE TREND for macro-sensitive upside, TKFEN (1W: +15.25% | 1M: -0.58%) RECOVERY for momentum follow-through, BIMAS (1W: +13.85% | 1M: +0.78%) ABOVE TREND for retail resilience, ASELS (1W: -3.37% | 1M: +6.88%) CORRECTION as a watch for pullback buying, SOKM (1W: -8.28% | 1M: -18.15%) BELOW TREND for downside risk.

12. Sectors to Watch Next Week?
Energy & Chemicals for continued cyclical leadership, Holding & Investment for consolidation bounce candidates, and Banking to gauge whether the sector capitulates or stabilizes. Monitor liquidity into cyclicals versus safe-yield rotation.

13. Key Levels & Triggers?
Watch market confirmation via a sustained break above the 20‑day moving average and weekly highs for continuation; a breach of the 50‑day/longer-term moving averages would flip the tone. External catalysts: global risk sentiment, FX/CBRT signals and corporate earnings will govern sector rotation.

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
Risk-on market with normal volatility and broad sector participation; cyclicals (energy, holdings, select industrials) lead while banks remain a clear weak spot. Opportunity lies in momentum recovery names (TKFEN, BIMAS, PETKM, TUPRS) but keep exposure disciplined given mixed one‑month confirmations and concentrated downside in financials.
